prompt:
Question: Which of the following passages is the original verbatim passage? The other passages are paraphrased versions of it.

Options:
A. On November 18, 2014, two Palestinian men from Jerusalem attacked congregants with axes, knives, and a gun in Kehilat Bnei Torah synagogue in the Har Nof neighborhood of Jerusalem. The killings resulted in the death of four dual-nationality worshippers and critically injured a responding Druze Israeli police officer. The police also shot the two attackers, and one male worshipper, who was unconscious, died 11 months later. While some initial reports suggested that the Popular Front for the Liber
B. On the morning of 18 November 2014, two Palestinian men from Jerusalem entered Kehilat Bnei Torah synagogue, in the Har Nof neighborhood of Jerusalem, and attacked the praying congregants with axes, knives, and a gun. They killed four dual-nationality worshippers, and critically wounded a responding Druze Israeli police officer, who later died of his wounds. They also injured seven male worshippers, one of whom never woke up from a coma and died 11 months later. The two attackers were then shot dead by the police. Several initial reports claimed that the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) had declared responsibility for the attack; Other sources say declarations by PFLP militants were either confused or that the group itself disclaimed responsibility. Israeli authorities stated that 'it seemed
C. On the 18th of November, 2014, in the morning hours, two Palestinian individuals originating from Jerusalem gained entry into the Kehilat Bnei Torah synagogue, situated in Jerusalem's Har Nof neighborhood. There, they assaulted the congregants who were engaged in prayer, employing axes, knives, and a firearm. The incident resulted in the fatalities of four worshippers holding dual nationalities. Additionally, a Druze Israeli police officer who arrived at the scene to respond was critically injured and subsequently succumbed to his wounds. Seven male worshippers also sustained injuries, with one among them never recovering from a coma and passing away 11 months afterward. The two assailants were subsequently shot and killed by law enforcement officers. Initial accounts from various sources indicated that the Popular Front for the Liberation of Palestine (PFLP) had taken credit for orchestrating the attack; however, other reports suggest that statements from PFLP militants were either unclear or that the organization itself denied involvement.
